name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Row
resistance_profile: Supported unilateral row through a long range; easy to train hard.
cues: Full stretch forward, row to the hip
execution_steps: Place one knee and the same-side hand on a bench, back flat and roughly parallel to the floor.
Hold a dumbbell in the other hand, arm hanging straight down in a full stretch.
Row the dumbbell up toward your hip, driving the elbow back without twisting the torso.
Squeeze the lat and upper back, then lower under control to the full stretch.
good_when: You want a supported, beginner-friendly row that spares the lower back.
You want to train each side independently to fix strength imbalances.
You only have dumbbells and a bench.
bad_when: You want to load both sides at once for time efficiency, where a barbell or machine row is faster.
